candidates.prompt: Chart NVIDIA Corporation's (NVDA) earnings surprises over the last 8 quarters — plot actual EPS vs estimated EPS as two lines over the 8 report dates, with the surprise delta (actualEarningResult minus epsEstimated) as bars on a secondary axis below, and color each quarter's bar green for beat / red for miss so I can read NVDA's beat streak at a glance.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_earnings mode=surprises symbol=NVDA limit=8 -> /stable/earnings returns date, actualEarningResult (EPS actual), epsEstimated, revenueActual, revenueEstimated, surpriseDelta per quarter; chart actual vs estimated EPS as dual lines + surpriseDelta bars over 8 quarters; beat/miss derived from sign of surpriseDelta.
candidates.chartIntent: Multi-series quarterly chart: x = 8 earnings dates, lines = actual EPS & estimated EPS, bars = surprise delta (dual-axis), beat/miss color-coded.

candidates.prompt: Compare the most recent quarterly EPS surprise delta across AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, and TSLA — rank all 7 mega-caps from biggest beat to biggest miss as a horizontal bar chart, with the surprise delta value ($) labeled on each bar and the revenue surprise % shown as a second overlaid series so I can see which giants are surprising on margin vs on the top line.
candidates.groundedEntity: AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, TSLA
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_earnings mode=surprises per symbol -> /stable/earnings returns surpriseDelta (latest quarter) plus revenueActual/revenueEstimated for revenue surprise %; rank 7 tickers by surprise delta; categorical bar chart with revenue surprise % as secondary series.
candidates.chartIntent: Ranked horizontal bar chart: x = 7 mega-cap tickers, primary bar = EPS surprise $, secondary series = revenue surprise %.

candidates.prompt: Plot Eli Lilly and Company's (LLY) quarterly revenue actual vs estimated and EPS actual vs estimated over the last 8 quarters as dual lines on two panels, and chart the revenue surprise % next to the EPS surprise % per quarter so I can see whether LLY's beats are driven by revenue strength or by margin/expansion.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_earnings mode=surprises symbol=LLY limit=8 -> /stable/earnings returns date, actualEarningResult, epsEstimated, revenueActual, revenueEstimated, surpriseDelta per quarter; compute revenue surprise % = (revenueActual - revenueEstimated) / revenueEstimated and EPS surprise % = surpriseDelta / epsEstimated; multi-series chart over 8 quarters.
candidates.chartIntent: Two-panel multi-series: top = revenue actual vs estimated lines, bottom = EPS actual vs estimated lines, with surprise % bars per quarter.

candidates.prompt: Aggregate Exxon Mobil Corporation's (XOM) insider Form 4 transactions over the last 90 days by week and chart the net dollar insider flow (sum of P-Purchase value minus sum of S-Sale value) per week as a bar chart over the 13 weeks, labeling which weeks insiders were net buyers (green) vs net sellers (red) and the cumulative net flow as a running line.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_insider_activity symbol=XOM limit=100 -> /stable/insider-trading/search returns filer, transactionType (P-Purchase / S-Sale), securities, value, date; aggregate value by ISO week and transactionType; net flow per week = sum(P value) - sum(S value); weekly bar chart + cumulative line over ~13 weeks.
candidates.chartIntent: Weekly time-series: x = 13 weeks, bars = net insider $ flow (green buy / red sell), line = cumulative net flow.

candidates.prompt: Pull Meta Platforms' (META) US congressional trades over the last 120 days and chart them by politician — one grouped bar per politician showing reported buy amount vs sell amount side by side — so I can see at a glance which House representatives and senators are most active in META and on which side of the trade.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_congressional_trades symbol=META chamber=both limit=100 -> /stable/house-trades and /stable/senate-trades return representative/senator, transactionDate, type (buy/sell), amount range, chamber; aggregate by politician and side; grouped bar chart per politician (buy vs sell).
candidates.chartIntent: Grouped bar chart: x = politicians who traded META, two bars each = buy $ vs sell $, sorted by total activity.

candidates.prompt: Rank AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, and TSLA by DCF upside % — (dcfPrice minus stockPrice) divided by stockPrice — as a horizontal bar chart from cheapest (most upside) to most expensive (most downside), with each ticker's EV/EBITDA multiple labeled on its bar so I can see which mega-caps look intrinsically undervalued versus which trade rich on enterprise multiples.
candidates.groundedEntity: AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, TSLA
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_valuation per symbol -> /stable/discounted-cash-flow (dcfPrice, stockPrice -> upside %) and /stable/enterprise-values (evOverEBITDA); rank 7 tickers by DCF upside; horizontal bar chart with EV/EBITDA annotated per ticker (dual magnitude: % vs multiple).
candidates.chartIntent: Ranked horizontal bar chart: x = 7 tickers, bar = DCF upside %, annotated label = EV/EBITDA multiple.

candidates.prompt: Plot Tesla, Inc.'s (TSLA) multi-period price changes (1W, 1M, 3M, 6M, YTD, 1Y) as bars alongside TSLA's EV/EBITDA and EV/Sales multiples as two lines on a secondary axis, so I can see whether TSLA's recent returns are supported by cheaper or richer enterprise multiples across each lookback window.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_valuation symbol=TSLA -> /stable/stock-price-change (1W/1M/3M/6M/YTD/1Y % returns) and /stable/enterprise-values (evOverEBITDA, evOverSales); dual-axis chart: returns % as bars (left axis) + EV/EBITDA & EV/Sales as lines (right axis) across 6 lookback windows.
candidates.chartIntent: Dual-axis multi-period chart: x = 6 lookback windows, bars = TSLA price-change %, lines = EV/EBITDA & EV/Sales.

candidates.prompt: Pull JPMorgan Chase & Co.'s (JPM) FMP peer group and rank JPM and each peer from cheapest to richest on DCF upside vs current price, with EV/EBITDA and the latest annual revenue growth % shown as side columns, then render the group as a bar chart sorted by DCF upside so I can read the relative-value table and the ranking chart together.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_peers symbol=JPM -> /stable/stock-peers returns peer symbols; per peer get_valuation -> /stable/discounted-cash-flow (dcfPrice vs stockPrice -> upside %) and /stable/enterprise-values (evOverEBITDA); get_growth per peer period=annual limit=1 -> /stable/financial-growth (growthRevenue); rank combined set cheapest-to-richest on DCF upside; metric table + sorted bar chart.
candidates.chartIntent: Ranked metric table + bar chart: rows = JPM + peers, columns = DCF upside %, EV/EBITDA, revenue growth %, sorted by DCF upside.

candidates.prompt: Rank all 11 GICS sectors (Technology, Healthcare, Financial Services, Energy, Consumer Cyclical, Consumer Defensive, Communication Services, Industrials, Utilities, Real Estate, Basic Materials) by YTD return as a bar chart, with each sector's average P/E overlaid as a line on a secondary axis, so I can see which sectors are leading on YTD return and whether their valuations look stretched or cheap relative to that performance.
candidates.groundedEntity: Technology, Healthcare, Financial Services, Energy, Consumer Cyclical, Consumer Defensive, Communication Services, Industrials, Utilities, Real Estate, Basic Materials
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_sector_performance -> /stable/sector-performance-snapshot (sectorPerformance: 1D/5D/1M/YTD per sector) and /stable/sector-pe-snapshot (sectorPe: average P/E per sector); rank 11 sectors by YTD; dual-axis bar+line chart (YTD % on left, P/E on right).
candidates.chartIntent: Dual-axis sector ranking: x = 11 GICS sectors sorted by YTD return, bars = YTD return %, line = average P/E.

candidates.prompt: Within the Technology sector, chart YTD performance across its industries (Semiconductors, Software - Infrastructure, Software - Application, Communication Equipment, Electronic Components, IT Services, Computer Hardware, Scientific & Technical Instruments, etc.) as a ranked bar chart from best to worst YTD return, labeling each industry's YTD % so I can see which Technology industries are leading the sector's rotation.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_sector_performance -> /stable/industry-performance-snapshot returns per-industry 1D/5D/1M/YTD; filter to industries whose parent sector = Technology; rank industries by YTD; categorical bar chart with YTD % labeled per bar.
candidates.chartIntent: Ranked bar chart: x = Technology industries, bar = YTD return %, sorted best-to-worst with labels.

candidates.prompt: Plot 5-day and 1-month returns for all 11 GICS sectors (Technology, Healthcare, Financial Services, Energy, Consumer Cyclical, Consumer Defensive, Communication Services, Industrials, Utilities, Real Estate, Basic Materials) as a grouped bar chart with two bars per sector, sorted by 1-month return, so I can see which sectors have short-term 5-day momentum building or fading relative to their 1-month trend.
candidates.groundedEntity: Technology, Healthcare, Financial Services, Energy, Consumer Cyclical, Consumer Defensive, Communication Services, Industrials, Utilities, Real Estate, Basic Materials
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_sector_performance -> /stable/sector-performance-snapshot returns 5D and 1M per sector for all 11 sectors; grouped bar chart (two bars per sector: 5D, 1M) sorted by 1M return.
candidates.chartIntent: Grouped bar chart: x = 11 sectors sorted by 1M return, two bars each = 5D return % and 1M return %.

candidates.prompt: Chart Apple Inc.'s (AAPL) most recent fiscal-year revenue split by geography (Americas, Europe, Greater China, Japan, Rest of Asia Pacific) as a grouped bar chart with the prior-year revenue per region shown as a second bar per region, and label the YoY % change on each region so I can see which geography is growing and which is shrinking.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_revenue_segments segment=geographic symbol=AAPL limit=20 -> /stable/revenue-geographic-segmentation returns revenue by region and date; pull the two most recent fiscal years per region; YoY delta per region; grouped bar chart (current vs prior year) with YoY % labels.
candidates.chartIntent: Grouped bar chart: x = 5 geographic regions, two bars each = current FY revenue vs prior FY revenue, YoY % labeled.

candidates.prompt: Plot Apple Inc.'s (AAPL) revenue by product line (iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables/Home/Accessories, Services) over the last 8 fiscal quarters as a multi-line chart, one line per product line, so I can see which product line is driving AAPL's growth and which is decelerating over the 8 quarters.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_revenue_segments segment=product symbol=AAPL limit=20 -> /stable/revenue-product-segmentation returns revenue by product line and date; pull 8 quarters per product line; multi-line chart (5 product lines over 8 quarters).
candidates.chartIntent: Multi-line chart: x = 8 fiscal quarters, 5 lines = revenue per product line (iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables, Services).

candidates.prompt: Chart Amazon.com, Inc.'s (AMZN) annual growth rates for revenue, EBITDA, free cash flow, and EPS over the last 8 fiscal years as a multi-line chart, and call out in the narrative whether AMZN's growth is accelerating or decelerating across each of the 4 metrics year over year.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_growth symbol=AMZN period=annual limit=8 -> /stable/financial-growth returns growthRevenue, growthEBITDA, growthFreeCashFlow, growthEPS by year; multi-line chart over 8 years (4 growth-rate series).
candidates.chartIntent: Multi-line chart: x = 8 fiscal years, 4 lines = revenue/EBITDA/FCF/EPS growth %.

candidates.prompt: Compare free float % and shares outstanding for AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, and TSLA as a grouped bar chart, then overlay each ticker's Altman Z-score and DCF upside % as two lines on a secondary axis, so I can see whether the most widely-held mega-caps also score well on quality and intrinsic value. Note explicitly if any 13F institutional holder data is unavailable at the current FMP tier rather than guessing.
candidates.groundedEntity: AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, TSLA
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_ownership per symbol -> /stable/shares-float (freeFloat, outstandingShares); get_quality_scores per symbol -> /stable/financial-scores (altmanZScore); get_valuation per symbol -> /stable/discounted-cash-flow (dcfPrice vs stockPrice -> upside %); grouped bar chart (float% + Z + DCF upside) across 7 tickers; 13F holder subsection surfaces partialErrors at current tier (avoids the 402-blocked holder chain).
candidates.chartIntent: Grouped/dual-axis chart: x = 7 mega-caps, bars = free float % & outstanding shares, lines = Altman Z & DCF upside %.

candidates.prompt: Build a one-pass investment-thesis view on JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM): pull JPM's free float %, shares outstanding, Altman Z / Piotroski F / Beneish M scores, and DCF fair value vs current price, then chart the three quality scores (Z, F, M) as bars with the DCF upside % labeled alongside, so I can read whether JPM's float structure is supported by quality and intrinsic value. State explicitly if the 13F institutional holder table is unavailable at the current FMP tier.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_ownership symbol=JPM -> /stable/shares-float (freeFloat, outstandingShares; 13F subsection surfaces partialErrors at current tier); get_quality_scores symbol=JPM -> /stable/financial-scores (altmanZScore, piotroskiScore, beneishMScore); get_valuation symbol=JPM -> /stable/discounted-cash-flow (dcfPrice, stockPrice -> upside %); bar chart of Z/F/M + DCF upside label.
candidates.chartIntent: Metric table + bar chart: rows = float/outstanding/Z/F/M/DCF upside, bars = the 3 quality scores with DCF upside % annotated.

candidates.prompt: Plot the current US Treasury yield curve across all maturities (1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, 2Y, 5Y, 10Y, 20Y, 30Y) as a line chart with each maturity's yield % labeled, and explicitly call out whether the curve is normal, flat, or inverted between the 2Y and 10Y maturities using the 2Y-10Y spread in basis points.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_macro_calendar -> /stable/treasury-rates returns yields by maturity (1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, 2Y, 5Y, 10Y, 20Y, 30Y); line chart of yield % vs maturity; 2Y-10Y spread = yield(10Y) - yield(2Y) in bps; classify normal/flat/inverted.
candidates.chartIntent: Yield-curve line chart: x = 9 maturities (short to long), y = yield %, with 2Y-10Y spread labeled in bps.

candidates.prompt: Over the next 14 days, chart every major US macro event (FOMC, CPI, Employment Situation, Retail Sales, PPI, ISM Manufacturing PMI, Consumer Confidence) on a date timeline as bars with each event's importance rating (High/Medium/Low) encoded as the bar height, so I can see which weeks are macro-heavy and which are quiet leading into the next FOMC and CPI prints.
candidates.groundedEntity: FOMC, CPI, Employment Situation, Retail Sales, PPI, ISM Manufacturing PMI, Consumer Confidence
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_macro_calendar from=today to=today+14d -> /stable/economic-calendar returns event, date, country, importance; filter to the 7 named high-impact US event types; timeline bar chart by date with importance (High=3/Medium=2/Low=1) as bar height.
candidates.chartIntent: Timeline bar chart: x = 14 calendar dates, bars = macro events on each date, bar height = importance rating, color-coded by event type.

candidates.prompt: Compare Altman Z-score, Piotroski F-score, and Beneish M-score across AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, and TSLA as a grouped bar chart with three bars per ticker (one per score), so I can see at a glance which mega-caps score highest on bankruptcy-distance quality and which carry the highest Beneish manipulation signal.
candidates.groundedEntity: AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, TSLA
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_quality_scores per symbol -> /stable/financial-scores returns altmanZScore, piotroskiScore, beneishMScore; grouped bar chart (3 scores per ticker) across 7 mega-caps.
candidates.chartIntent: Grouped bar chart: x = 7 mega-cap tickers, 3 bars each = Altman Z, Piotroski F, Beneish M.

candidates.prompt: For Eli Lilly and Company (LLY), chart the Altman Z, Piotroski F, and Beneish M scores as bars against their distress thresholds (Altman Z safe >3.0 / distress <1.8; Piotroski F high >7 / low <5; Beneish M safe >-1.78 / manipulator <-2.22) shown as horizontal reference lines, and label LLY's FMP rating grade alongside, so I can read at a glance whether LLY sits in the quality zone or the distress zone on each score.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_quality_scores symbol=LLY -> /stable/financial-scores (altmanZScore, piotroskiScore, beneishMScore) and /stable/ratings-snapshot (FMP rating grade); bar chart of 3 scores with distress/safe threshold reference lines per score and the FMP rating labeled.
candidates.chartIntent: Bar chart with reference lines: x = 3 quality scores (Z, F, M), y = score value, horizontal lines = safe/distress thresholds, FMP rating annotated.

candidates.prompt: Plot Johnson & Johnson's (JNJ) quarterly dividend per share over the last 5 years (~20 quarters) as a step chart, labeling each ex-dividend date and the dividend amount on the step, so I can see JNJ's dividend growth trajectory, cadence, and any hike dates over the 5-year window.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_dividends mode=history symbol=JNJ limit=20 -> /stable/dividends returns date (ex-div), dividend, recordDate, paymentDate, declarationDate; step chart of dividend per share by date over ~20 quarters with amount labels.
candidates.chartIntent: Step chart: x = ~20 ex-dividend dates over 5 years, y = dividend $ per share, each step labeled with amount.

candidates.prompt: Count Costco Wholesale Corporation's (COST) SEC filings over the last 120 days by formType (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, DEF 14A, 424B4, SC 13D) and chart the count per form type as a bar chart, then list the filing dates of any 8-K material events and 10-K annual reports underneath the chart so I can see COST's recent disclosure mix and the material-event dates in one view.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_sec_filings symbol=COST limit=100 -> /stable/sec-filings-search/symbol returns formType, filingDate, link, finalLink over the last 120 days; aggregate count by formType; categorical bar chart of counts + 8-K/10-K filing-date list below.
candidates.chartIntent: Bar chart + sub-list: x = SEC form types (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, DEF 14A, 424B4, SC 13D), y = filing count over 120 days, with 8-K/10-K dates listed.

candidates.prompt: List every S&P 500 (^GSPC) constituent classified in the Semiconductors sub-industry and chart them by market cap as a horizontal bar chart sorted largest to smallest, with each ticker's sector and sub-industry labeled, so I can see the universe of large-cap semiconductor names in the S&P 500 at a glance.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_index_constituents index=sp500 -> /stable/sp500-constituent returns symbol, name, sector, subSector per member; filter to subSector=Semiconductors; get_stock_quote symbols=[semis] -> /stable/batch-quote returns marketCap per ticker; rank by market cap; horizontal bar chart with sector/sub-industry labels.
candidates.chartIntent: Ranked horizontal bar chart: x = S&P 500 semiconductor tickers, bar = market cap, sorted largest-to-smallest, sub-industry labeled.

candidates.prompt: Pull the Dow Jones Industrial Average (^DJI) 30 constituents and chart them grouped by GICS sector as a bar chart of constituent count per sector, labeling each ticker inside its sector group, so I can see the sector composition and concentration of the Dow 30 at a glance.
candidates.answerabilityNote: get_index_constituents index=dowjones -> /stable/dowjones-constituent returns symbol, name, sector, subSector for all 30 Dow members; count constituents per GICS sector; categorical bar chart of count per sector with ticker labels per group.
candidates.chartIntent: Sector-composition bar chart: x = GICS sectors represented in the Dow 30, y = constituent count, each bar labeled with its tickers.

candidates.prompt: Plot Exxon Mobil Corporation's (XOM) daily closing price over the last 12 months (~252 trading days) as a line chart with the 50-day and 200-day simple moving averages overlaid as two additional lines, so I can see XOM's price trend over the year and whether XOM is currently trading above or below its key 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

candidates.chartIntent: Multi-line time chart: x = ~252 trading days, 3 lines = XOM close, SMA50, SMA200.
